# Attention mechanisms

Self-attention variants, from vanilla causal MHA to compressive-memory and per-depth-biased variants.

Registry: ``praxis.ATTENTION_REGISTRY`` (7 entries)

## `arc` - ArcAttention

InfiniAttention subclass that adds per-depth learned biases.

Source: [praxis/attention/arc.py:19](../praxis/attention/arc.py#L19)

## `causal` - CausalAttention

Causal self-attention using PyTorch's FlexAttention API. Provides efficient attention
computation with customizable block masking. Supports optional sliding window for
efficient long-sequence inference.

Source: [praxis/attention/causal.py:19](../praxis/attention/causal.py#L19)

## `infini` - InfiniAttention

CausalAttention subclass that adds segment-level compressive memory.

Source: [praxis/attention/infini.py:69](../praxis/attention/infini.py#L69)

## `pk` - ProductKeyAttention

Basically stolen from here: https://github.com/lucidrains/PEER-
pytorch/blob/main/PEER_pytorch/PK.py

Source: [praxis/attention/pk_attention.py:20](../praxis/attention/pk_attention.py#L20)

## `standard` - ModularAttention

This class is akin to a wrapper, which implements a number of interesting attention
mechanisms, and makes them optional with feature flags. By toggling features, one can
essentially blend components from various kinds of attention.

Source: [praxis/attention/base.py:29](../praxis/attention/base.py#L29)

## `syntaxes` - SyntaxesAttention

Syntaxes Attention: All queries attend to reduced K/V context.

Source: [praxis/attention/syntaxes.py:13](../praxis/attention/syntaxes.py#L13)

## `vanilla` - VanillaMHA

Standard multi-head attention implementation using PyTorch's nn.MultiheadAttention.

Source: [praxis/attention/components.py:397](../praxis/attention/components.py#L397)
